description: Quality evaluation for skill-social-copywriter. Tests output quality on real use cases based on the user's actual context. Brand context is loaded from skill-context. Platform rules are loaded from skill-platform-knowledge.
primary_use_case: Threads (Meta) — storytelling thread about app development — engagement and community objective

test_cases.name: Core use case — storytelling thread from scratch
test_cases.description: The most common task: write a storytelling thread about the app from a topic given by the user.

input.prompt: Scrivi un thread su Threads che racconta perché ho deciso di iniziare a sviluppare la mia app. Voglio che sia personale e che faccia venire voglia di seguire il progetto.

assertions.check: The first post stops the scroll — it is specific, personal, and creates curiosity. It does not start with 'Oggi vi racconto' or generic openers.
Thread has between 5 and 10 posts. Each post contains one idea only. Last post has a CTA.
Each post is within 500 characters. No external links inside the thread body. Hashtags only in last post (1-3 max).
Copy matches the TOV defined in skill-context. Does not sound corporate or generic. Feels personal and authentic.
Thread has a clear narrative arc: situation → tension/challenge → resolution or ongoing journey. Reader is carried forward.
Thread ends with a question or CTA that invites replies and discussion. Does not end abruptly.
Each post is clearly labeled (Post 1, Post 2, etc.). Technical notes are provided (character count, hashtags).

test_cases.name: Behind the scenes thread — development process
test_cases.description: Thread showing what happens behind the scenes while building the app. Common format for community building.

input.prompt: Crea un thread behind the scenes su una feature che sto costruendo in questo momento. Non posso dire di che feature si tratta, ma voglio mostrare il processo di sviluppo.

assertions.check: The skill works with the constraint (no feature name revealed) and builds intrigue around the mystery rather than asking to reveal it.
Copy feels like a real founder/developer talking, not a marketing team. Includes specific details, doubts, or micro-moments that make it believable.
Each post within 500 characters. Structure follows thread best practices from skill-platform-knowledge.
Thread invites the audience into the process — asks for opinions, feedback, or reactions. Not a one-way broadcast.
At least 1-2 posts end with a micro-cliffhanger or teaser that pulls the reader to the next post.

test_cases.name: Milestone announcement thread
test_cases.description: Sharing a meaningful milestone in the app development journey. Tests ability to balance celebration with community engagement.

input.prompt: Ho appena raggiunto il primo milestone importante nello sviluppo della mia app. Voglio condividerlo su Threads in modo che la community si senta parte del percorso.

assertions.check: The two variants use genuinely different narrative approaches — not just rephrased versions of the same idea.
Both variants make the audience feel part of the achievement — uses 'noi', 'insieme', 'grazie a chi segue', or equivalent. Not just self-congratulatory.
Both variants comply with 500 char limit per post and thread structure best practices.
At least one variant uses vulnerability or honest reflection (what was hard, what was unexpected) rather than just celebrating the win.
Each variant is clearly labeled with its approach (e.g. 'Variant 1 — Storytelling / Variant 2 — Contrarian'). Brief explanation of creative choice is provided.

test_cases.name: Lesson learned thread — mistake or pivot
test_cases.description: Tests ability to write honest, vulnerable content about a mistake or change of direction. High-engagement format for builders.

input.prompt: Ho cambiato direzione su una decisione importante per la mia app. Voglio scrivere un thread onesto su cosa non ha funzionato e cosa ho imparato.

assertions.check: Copy is honest and specific about what went wrong, but frames it as a learning — not a complaint or an excuse.
Thread includes at least one concrete detail (a decision, a moment, a realization) rather than staying vague and generic.
500 char limit per post respected. Thread structure follows best practices.
Thread ends with a clear lesson or insight the audience can apply or reflect on — not just 'keep going'.
Last post asks the audience a question related to the lesson — invites them to share their own experience.

test_cases.name: Missing mother skill — fallback behavior
test_cases.description: Tests that the skill correctly stops and alerts the user when skill-context or skill-platform-knowledge is not active.

input.prompt: Scrivi un thread sulla mia app

assertions.check: Claude does NOT produce any copy. It stops at Step 0.
Claude tells the user exactly which skill is missing (skill-context) and where to activate it.
Claude offers the user option (b): provide brand context inline for this session only.

grading_scale.critical: Must pass. Failure here means the skill is broken.
grading_scale.high: Should pass. Failure here means the skill needs improvement before use.
grading_scale.medium: Nice to have. Failure here is a refinement opportunity.
grading_scale.low: Polish. Does not affect core functionality.

pass_threshold.high: 80% — at least 4 out of 5 high-weight assertions must pass per test case
pass_threshold.overall: If any critical assertion fails, the test case fails regardless of other scores
